name: Beef Burgundy
recipeIngredient: 4 lbs Beef chuck; cut into 1-1/2-inch cubes; fat trimmed-off
2 cups Burgundy or other dry red table wine
1 Sprig fresh thyme or 1 tsp Dried thyme
recipeInstructions:
1. Brown onions with bacon and butter in a Dutch oven.
2. Remove onions and bacon with a slotted spoon and set aside.
3. Add meat to the pan and brown well on all sides.
4. If desired, pour brandy over beef and tilt the pan to keep the flame going as long as possible (note: I never flame the brandy; instead, add the brandy when adding the wine).
5. Add wine, garlic, mushrooms, water, onions, and bacon back into the pan.
6. Make a bouquet garni by tying together parsley, celery top, carrot, bay leaf, and thyme in cheesecloth (use a long string for easy removal).
7. Add the bouquet garni to the pan.
8. Cover and simmer for about 1-1/2 hours, or until the meat is tender.
9. Lift beef, mushrooms, and onions out of the pan with a slotted spoon; arrange in a covered 3-quart casserole or baking dish.
10. Strain the liquid through a sieve, discarding the bouquet garni, garlic, and bacon.
11. Mix flour to a smooth paste with cold water; stir into the meat stock and cook until the gravy is thick and smooth.
12. Pour the gravy over the meat and serve immediately, or refrigerate and reheat, covered, in a moderate oven at 350°F for about 35 minutes, or until hot and bubbly. Makes 8 to 10 servings.
